page: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v7 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the Easv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. This configuration features 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs with hyper-threading enabled, running at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 768.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 16.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The server does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, but it is Premium Storage capable.

In benchmark testing, the server demonstrates strong computational and memory capabilities. Single-core performance is particularly notable, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance, gzip compression, and bzip3 decompression all scoring in the top 10% tier. Multi-core CPU performance and PassMark CPU scores fall within the top 25% tier. Memory subsystem performance is highlighted by a top-tier PassMark memory score and strong write bandwidth, though read-only large block memory bandwidth remains in the average tier.

From a resource density perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads by reducing the need to provision excess compute cores simply to acquire sufficient RAM. However, the lack of local storage requires reliance on external Premium Storage, which introduces network-based storage latency tradeoffs. Additionally, the absence of hardware accelerators limits its utility for heavy parallel processing tasks, although it achieves top-tier text generation speeds for small language models.

This server is configured for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching layers. Factual benchmark data supports its suitability for Redis SET operations and database transactions, both of which scored in the top 25% tier. It is also appropriate for static web serving and memory-heavy enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or GPU acceleration.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server from the Easv7 family, featuring a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 24 physical cores, 48 vCPUs, and a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is provisioned with 768.0 GB of RAM, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 16.0 GB per core. The instance lacks local storage and GPUs but supports Premium Storage. Benchmarks indicate top-tier single-core CPU and memory performance, alongside strong multi-core and database transaction capabilities. Large-block memory read bandwidth remains average, representing a minor tradeoff. This server is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-heavy workloads, minimizing the need to over-provision compute resources. It is suited for database operations, Redis caching, static web serving, and memory-intensive enterprise applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v7 is an x86_64 memory-optimized server featuring a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC processor, 48 vCPUs, and 768 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v7 is an x86_64 server with 48 vCPUs, 768 GB RAM, and a 4.5 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A high-density memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processing and exceptional single-core performance for memory-bound enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor with 48 vCPUs and 4.5 GHz clock speed
768.0 GB of system memory providing a high 16.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and memory benchmark performance
No local storage or GPU, requiring external Premium Storage
Suited for database operations, Redis caching, and memory-intensive applications
